explanationZhTw: 「devised」在這裡是「設計、構思並安排出來」的意思，指 Newspeak 不是自然形成，而是被刻意創造出來以達成特定目的。
generatedExample: The curriculum was devised to help beginners learn faster.

explanationZhTw: 「ideological」指「意識形態的」，也就是和某種政治或思想立場有關，不只是一般實用需求。
generatedExample: The speech was full of ideological arguments.

explanationZhTw: 「sole means of communication」表示「唯一的溝通方式」，其中「sole」強調只有這一種選項。
generatedExample: For a week, my phone was my sole means of communication.

explanationZhTw: 「tour de force」是慣用語，指「令人驚嘆的高難度傑作」或「非常厲害的表現」，通常帶有佩服的語氣。
generatedExample: Her performance was a real tour de force.

explanationZhTw: 「specialist」是「專家、專精某一領域的人」。這裡表示這件事太難，只有熟練的專家才能完成。
generatedExample: You should see a specialist if the pain continues.

explanationZhTw: 「superseded」在這裡是「取代、接替而使原本的東西過時」；不是單純超越，而是新者把舊者換掉。
generatedExample: Digital files have superseded paper records in many offices.

explanationZhTw: 「gained ground」是片語動詞，表示「逐漸普及、越來越被接受」。
generatedExample: The new idea is gaining ground among voters.

explanationZhTw: 「embodied」在這裡不是身體上的「體現」，而是指「具體載於、收錄在」某個版本或文件中。
generatedExample: The rule is embodied in the company handbook.

explanationZhTw: 「provisional」表示「暫定的、臨時的」，通常暗示之後還會修改。
generatedExample: We only have a provisional schedule for next week.

explanationZhTw: 「superfluous」是「多餘的、不必要的」；在這裡指那些應該被刪掉的字詞。
generatedExample: Please remove the superfluous details from the report.

explanationZhTw: 「archaic formations」指「古舊、過時的詞形或結構」；這裡是在說有些字詞形式很老派，之後會被淘汰。
generatedExample: The manuscript still contains a few archaic formations.

explanationZhTw: 「suppressed」在這裡表示「被壓制、被刪除或禁止」，不是單純「壓住」而已，而是指之後會被消除。
generatedExample: The authorities suppressed the false rumor quickly.

explanationZhTw: 《新語》是《1984》中的虛構語言，這裡不是一般的「新說法」，而是帶有政治控制目的的專有名詞。
generatedExample: In the novel, Newspeak is designed to narrow what people can even think.

explanationZhTw: `medium of expression` 指「表達的媒介／方式」，這裡是說語言本身被當作表達思想的工具。
generatedExample: For many artists, painting is a medium of expression.

explanationZhTw: `world-view` 是「世界觀」，指一個人或群體看待世界與理解事物的基本觀點。
generatedExample: Her world-view changed after she lived abroad for a year.

explanationZhTw: `devotees` 指「信奉者、擁護者」，這裡特別指對伊諾克社會主義（Ingsoc）忠誠的人。
generatedExample: The devotees of the movement believed its ideas could change society.

explanationZhTw: `once and for all` 是慣用語，表示「徹底地、永久地、一次解決不再反覆」。
generatedExample: We need to settle the issue once and for all.

explanationZhTw: `heretical` 原本指「異端的」，在這裡是指違反官方意識形態、被視為危險或不可接受的思想。
generatedExample: In that system, even a small criticism could be treated as heretical.

explanationZhTw: `diverging from` 在這裡是「偏離、違背」，指思想不符合 Ingsoc 的原則。
generatedExample: Any opinion diverging from the party line was quickly suppressed.

explanationZhTw: `unthinkable` 這裡不是單純「想不到」，而是指「在語言和制度下根本無法被思考／表達」。
generatedExample: The idea was once unthinkable, but now it is widely accepted.

explanationZhTw: `strip ... of` 是片語動詞，表示「剝除、去掉」。這裡是把詞語原本不符合正統的含義去掉。
generatedExample: The revised version strips the slogan of any political meaning.

explanationZhTw: `secondary meanings` 指「引申義、次要含義」；這裡說要把詞語除了字面意思以外的其他聯想也一併清除。
generatedExample: The editor asked us to remove any secondary meanings that might confuse readers.

explanationZhTw: 這裡 `free from` 不是「自由」，而是表示「沒有、免於」，如「沒有跳蚤／雜草」。
generatedExample: This jacket is free from harmful chemicals.

explanationZhTw: `free from` 在這裡表示「沒有」，整句是指田地沒有雜草。
generatedExample: The garden is free from weeds after the rain.

explanationZhTw: `politically free` 在這裡是指「政治上自由的」，也就是擁有政治自主權。
generatedExample: People in the country were no longer politically free.

explanationZhTw: `intellectually free` 指「思想上自由的」，也就是能自由思考、不受限制。
generatedExample: A good university should help students feel intellectually free.

explanationZhTw: `of necessity` 是較正式的說法，意思是「必然地、理所當然地」。
generatedExample: If the rules disappear, the category becomes of necessity unclear.

explanationZhTw: `an end in itself` 指「本身就是目的」，不是為了別的結果而做。
generatedExample: For some people, reading is an end in itself.

explanationZhTw: `dispensed with` 是片語，意思是「可以省去、可以不要」。
generatedExample: In this version, any unnecessary step has been dispensed with.

explanationZhTw: `diminish` 是「減少、縮小」，這裡特指縮小人們可思考的範圍。
generatedExample: The policy would diminish the choices available to customers.

explanationZhTw: `cut ... down to a minimum` 是片語，表示「把……減到最少」。
generatedExample: They cut the budget down to a minimum.

explanationZhTw: 在這裡表示「建立在……基礎上／以……為基礎」，不是字面上的「創立於」。
generatedExample: The new grammar is founded on simple rules.

explanationZhTw: 表示「可理解的、聽得懂的／讀得懂的」，語氣較正式。
generatedExample: Her explanation was clear and intelligible.

explanationZhTw: 表示「明顯不同的類別／分組」，這裡強調三組詞彙彼此分開。
generatedExample: The students were sorted into distinct classes by level.

explanationZhTw: 用來介紹名稱，意思是「被稱為／叫作」。
generatedExample: This plant is known as lavender.

explanationZhTw: 指「複合詞」，也就是由兩個或以上的字組成的單字。
generatedExample: Homework and toothpaste are compound words.

explanationZhTw: 表示「處理；論及；在文中加以說明」。
generatedExample: The issue will be dealt with in the next meeting.

explanationZhTw: 表示「專門用來……／專注於……」。
generatedExample: This chapter is devoted to climate change.

explanationZhTw: 表示「仍然成立／依然適用」，是較正式、偏古風的用法。
generatedExample: The same principle held good in every case.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `business` 不是「生意」，而是「事務、用途」；整個片語指「日常生活所需的事務」。
generatedExample: Her job is to handle the business of everyday life, from bills to groceries.

explanationZhTw: `putting on` 是片語動詞，意思是「穿上」衣物。
generatedExample: She was putting on her coat when I called.

explanationZhTw: 這是慣用說法，表示「等等、以及類似的東西」。
generatedExample: I like simple foods like rice, noodles, and the like.

explanationZhTw: 這裡表示詞義被「非常嚴格地限定」，沒有彈性或可延伸的空間。
generatedExample: The rules were rigidly defined, so there was no room for interpretation.

explanationZhTw: `purged out of` 在這裡指「徹底清除、去除掉」；語氣比一般的 remove 更強。
generatedExample: The editor purged all vague phrases out of the report.

explanationZhTw: 這是較正式的說法，意思接近「就……而言」或「在……範圍內」。
generatedExample: So far as I can tell, the plan is still on track.

explanationZhTw: `staccato` 原指音樂上「斷奏」；這裡形容聲音短促、斷斷續續。
generatedExample: He answered in a staccato voice, as if he were out of breath.

explanationZhTw: `purposive` 表示「有目的的、帶著明確意圖的」。
generatedExample: Her actions were purposive, not random.

explanationZhTw: 可互換性；這裡指不同詞類之間幾乎可以彼此替換使用。
generatedExample: The interchangeability of the two labels confused new users.

explanationZhTw: 詞性；像名詞、動詞、形容詞、副詞這類語法分類。
generatedExample: In English, parts of speech can change the meaning of a sentence.

explanationZhTw: 抽象詞語；這裡指像 IF、WHEN 這種概念性、非具體的字。
generatedExample: She used an abstract word that was hard for children to picture.

explanationZhTw: 在此充當、代替；表示 THINK 同時扮演名詞和動詞的角色。
generatedExample: This old phone did duty for a camera on the trip.

explanationZhTw: 詞源原則；依字詞的來源或歷史演變來作選擇的原則。
generatedExample: The teacher explained the etymological principle behind the spelling.

explanationZhTw: 保留；指把原來的形式留下來，不加以刪除或替換。
generatedExample: The museum keeps the old labels for retention in the archive.

explanationZhTw: 在此是「刪除、停用」的意思，不是一般的「壓制」；表示某個詞被刻意不用。
generatedExample: The editor suppressed the repeated sentence to make the paragraph shorter.

explanationZhTw: 後綴 -FUL；加在字尾的構詞成分，用來形成新的詞。
generatedExample: Add the suffix -FUL to the base word to make an adjective.

explanationZhTw: 形容詞性的；與形容詞有關，或具有形容詞功能的。
generatedExample: The adjectival phrase gave the sentence a softer tone.

explanationZhTw: 不變的、固定的；這裡指 -WISE 的結尾不能改變。
generatedExample: The deadline was invariable, so everyone had to finish on time.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `negatived` 不是一般口語裡常見的用法，而是表示「被否定、被轉成相反意思」。也就是把原本的詞做成反義化。
generatedExample: In this system, a word can be negatived by adding a special prefix.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `affix` 指「加在字首或字尾的詞綴」。在這段話中，它特別指像 `UN-` 這種前綴，用來改變字的意思。
generatedExample: The prefix `un-` is an affix that can change a word's meaning.

explanationZhTw: 這是 Newspeak 裡虛構的加強前綴，表示「更、極度、超級」的程度，比 `PLUS-` 還強。
generatedExample: They used `DOUBLEPLUS-` to make the adjective sound extreme.

explanationZhTw: `prepositional affixes` 指像介系詞一樣能附加在字上的詞綴，例如 `ANTE-`、`POST-`、`UP-`、`DOWN-`。這裡是在說可用這種方式改變字義。
generatedExample: The language uses prepositional affixes to shift the meaning of many words.

explanationZhTw: `bring about` 是片語動詞，意思是「造成、促成、導致」。這裡指這些方法造成了詞彙的大幅減少。
generatedExample: The new policy brought about a big change in the school.

explanationZhTw: `diminution` 是較正式的字，意思是「減少、縮小、降低」。在這裡是指詞彙量的大幅縮減。
generatedExample: There was a noticeable diminution in noise after midnight.

explanationZhTw: 「區分特徵／顯著標誌」；這裡指《新話》文法的一個主要特色。
generatedExample: Her red scarf was the distinguishing mark that helped me spot her.

explanationZhTw: 「受……限制；在……條件下」；在這裡是說「除了少數例外之外」。
generatedExample: Subject to approval, the plan will start next month.

explanationZhTw: 文法術語，指「詞形變化」；例如動詞、名詞的變化形式。
generatedExample: The lesson focused on verb inflexions in English.

explanationZhTw: 文法術語，指「過去式」；這裡特別是在講動詞的過去式形式。
generatedExample: The preterite of "go" is irregular.

explanationZhTw: 「被廢除；被取消使用」；這裡指那些不規則形式被整個從語言中移除。
generatedExample: The old rule was abolished after the new policy took effect.

explanationZhTw: 成語，表示「視情況而定；看具體是哪一種情況」。
generatedExample: Use a spoon or a fork, as the case might be.

explanationZhTw: 「總是；不變地；一律地」；強調沒有例外。
generatedExample: She invariably arrives five minutes early.

explanationZhTw: 「被壓制；被取消；被禁止使用」；這裡是指不規則形式被刻意移除。
generatedExample: The report was suppressed to avoid public criticism.

explanationZhTw: 在文法中指「變化詞形」；這裡是指詞語依人稱、數、時態等做不規則變化。
generatedExample: Some verbs inflect differently in the past tense.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是「親屬」，而是文法中的關係詞，像 who、which、that 這類字。
generatedExample: In this lesson, the relatives are used to connect two clauses.

explanationZhTw: 助動詞，例如 be、have、will，用來幫助構成時態、語氣或否定。
generatedExample: The auxiliary verbs help form the question.

explanationZhTw: scrap 在這裡是「廢除、捨棄」；意思是因為沒必要而被取消。
generatedExample: The old rule was scrapped as unnecessary.

explanationZhTw: 這裡表示「刪除、取消」某些時態用法。
generatedExample: They dropped the extra step to save time.

explanationZhTw: 這裡表示原本的功能由後面的詞來承擔或取代。
generatedExample: Her travel costs are covered by the company.

explanationZhTw: 構詞法，指單字如何組成、變化或衍生出新詞。
generatedExample: Word-formation can reveal how a language grows.

explanationZhTw: 表示「由於、因...而產生」；這裡說詞形變化的問題是因為語速需求而起。
generatedExample: The misunderstanding arose out of a small translation error.

explanationZhTw: 表示「很可能會、容易會」發生某事。
generatedExample: This kind of message is liable to be misunderstood.

explanationZhTw: 表示「被認為是」；這裡是被視為壞詞。
generatedExample: The comment was held to be disrespectful.

explanationZhTw: 拉丁語片語，意思是「由此可知、因此本身就」；語氣正式。
generatedExample: If the policy is unfair, it is ipso facto hard to defend.

explanationZhTw: 表示「為了...起見、為了...方便」；這裡是為了讀音順口。
generatedExample: For the sake of clarity, I will keep this example short.

explanationZhTw: 指「悅耳、順口的發音」；這裡強調字音要好聽。
generatedExample: The poet chose the word for its euphony.

explanationZhTw: archaic 指「古老的、過時的」；這裡是古舊的詞形或構詞方式。
generatedExample: The text preserves an archaic formation that modern speakers rarely use.

explanationZhTw: 在這裡是固定搭配，表示「被賦予、被看重」；importance was attached to = 重要性被放在...上。
generatedExample: Great importance was attached to accuracy in the report.

explanationZhTw: 有意設計／刻意構造，強調這些詞不是自然形成，而是為了特定目的打造出來的。
generatedExample: The course was deliberately constructed to guide beginners step by step.

explanationZhTw: 政治含義；這裡指詞語背後帶有政治立場、效果或聯想，不只是字面意思。
generatedExample: Her comment had a political implication that some listeners noticed immediately.

explanationZhTw: 強加；把某種想法、規則或態度施加到別人身上。
generatedExample: The new slogan was meant to impose a single way of thinking on the public.

explanationZhTw: 理想的心理態度；這裡的 attitude 指心態或立場，不是單純的禮貌態度。
generatedExample: The coach tried to encourage a desirable mental attitude before the match.

explanationZhTw: 正確地；在這裡是指符合這套語言規則或用法地使用。
generatedExample: If you do not know the rule, you may not use the phrase correctly.

explanationZhTw: 改述；用較長或不同的話把原意重新說一遍。
generatedExample: She asked me to paraphrase the paragraph in simpler language.

explanationZhTw: 弦外之音／潛在含意；這裡不是音樂中的泛音，而是文字帶出的隱含色彩。
generatedExample: His apology had overtones of sarcasm, so nobody fully trusted it.

explanationZhTw: 語言縮寫式表達；用很少的字把很多意思壓縮進去。
generatedExample: In our team, emojis became a kind of verbal shorthand for quick feedback.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是「濃縮、塞進」的意思，不是裝箱。
generatedExample: The headline was packing several arguments into one short sentence.

explanationZhTw: 有力的、強而有說服力的；這裡不是指「使用武力的」字面意思。
generatedExample: Her explanation was brief but forcible enough to change my mind.

explanationZhTw: 複合詞，指把兩個或多個詞結合成一個新詞的字詞形式。
generatedExample: English has many compound words like 'toothbrush' and 'notebook'.

explanationZhTw: 縮寫；把較長的詞或片語簡化成較短的形式。
generatedExample: We often use abbreviations like 'TV' and 'UN' in everyday writing.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是比喻用法，表示「緊密地拼接在一起」；不是字面上的焊接。
generatedExample: The designer welded together different ideas into one clear concept.

explanationZhTw: 混合體；由不同部分融合成的一個整體。
generatedExample: The song is an amalgam of jazz, pop, and electronic sounds.

explanationZhTw: 詞形變化；依文法規則改變字形以表示時態、人稱、性數等。
generatedExample: In Spanish, verbs are inflected differently for each subject.

explanationZhTw: 在這裡指「正統思想」或「符合官方標準的觀點」，不是只指宗教上的正統。
generatedExample: The policy was defended as a form of orthodoxy within the organization.

explanationZhTw: 指「詞源的」；和字詞的歷史來源、字形來源有關。
generatedExample: She traced the etymological roots of the word back to Latin.

explanationZhTw: 在這裡是「由……組成、構成」；不是「編造」的意思。
generatedExample: The soup was made up of beans, carrots, and rice.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是「殘害」人的意思，而是指把字詞任意改形、切碎或破壞。
generatedExample: The quote was mutilated so badly that the original meaning disappeared.

explanationZhTw: 固定用法，意思是「例如」。
generatedExample: You can improve the layout, for instance by adding more white space.

explanationZhTw: 在兩個事物中指「後者」。
generatedExample: I can visit on Monday or Friday, and I prefer the latter.

explanationZhTw: 指語音上的悅耳順口；這裡是「好聽、好發音」的意思。
generatedExample: The author chose the name partly for its euphony.

explanationZhTw: 指不規則的構詞或變化形式，沒有統一規則。
generatedExample: This language has many irregular formations in its verb forms.

explanationZhTw: 表示「依序分別地對應」。
generatedExample: The players were Alice, Ben, and Cara, respectively, in first, second, and third place.

explanationZhTw: 指詞形變化，例如時態、數、格或人稱的變化。
generatedExample: Many nouns inflect for number in this language.

explanationZhTw: 指詞形變化，例如時態、數、格或人稱的變化。
generatedExample: Many nouns inflect for number in this language.

explanationZhTw: 指「被細微區分、被刻意精緻化到很難直接理解的意思」。這裡不是一般的「subtle」，而是表示語意被做得非常精細、複雜，普通人難以把握。
generatedExample: Her reply had subtilized meanings that only the team lead understood.

explanationZhTw: 「幾乎聽不懂／幾乎無法理解」。這裡強調的是可理解程度非常低。
generatedExample: The lecture was barely intelligible because the audio kept cutting out.

explanationZhTw: 在這裡指「譯法、表達方式、轉述版本」，不是一般的「渲染」。
generatedExample: This rendering sounds more natural than the literal translation.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是「理解、領會」的意思，常見但在此搭配 the full meaning，表示要徹底弄懂。
generatedExample: I can grasp the idea, but I still need more examples.

explanationZhTw: 意思是「以……為基礎／對……有扎實理解」。這裡指只有深受英社思想浸染的人才能懂。
generatedExample: Her advice is grounded in years of field experience.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是「完整的效果／全部威力」的意思，不是字面上的力量。
generatedExample: You only feel the full force of the argument when you read the whole essay.

explanationZhTw: 指「盲目而熱烈的接受」。這是描述某種不經思考、完全服從的態度。
generatedExample: The slogan encouraged blind, enthusiastic acceptance of the policy.

explanationZhTw: 意思是「不可分割地混在一起、緊密糾纏在一起」。這裡強調 OLDTHINK 和「邪惡、墮落」幾乎綁在一起。
generatedExample: His reputation is inextricably mixed up with that scandal.

explanationZhTw: 指「被丟棄、作廢並遺忘」。這裡是把不再需要的詞彙直接淘汰。
generatedExample: Once the system was redesigned, the old labels were scrapped and forgotten.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是單純「確定一下」，而是「確認清楚、保證明白」。
generatedExample: Before publishing, make sure the wording is consistent.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是單純「確定一下」，而是「確認清楚、保證明白」。
generatedExample: Before publishing, make sure the wording is consistent.

explanationZhTw: 「為了方便起見」；這裡表示只是出於使用上的便利而保留舊詞。
generatedExample: We kept the old label for the sake of convenience.

explanationZhTw: 「不再存在／消失」；這裡指這些詞已被從語言中刪除。
generatedExample: After the merger, the old department names ceased to exist.

explanationZhTw: 「概括性用語／包山包海的詞」；在此指一個詞就能涵蓋一整類概念。
generatedExample: The report used blanket words instead of precise terms.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是單純「覆蓋」，而是「把它們統統包含進去」。
generatedExample: One category covered them all, so we did not need separate labels.

explanationZhTw: 「把它們廢除掉／抹去」；指這些詞被語言系統直接消滅。
generatedExample: The new rules abolished them overnight.

explanationZhTw: 「觀點／思維方式」；這裡指黨員應有的整體思想姿態。
generatedExample: Her outlook changed after she moved to another country.

explanationZhTw: 「正統信仰／正統觀點」；此處指符合黨規的思想正確性。
generatedExample: He was punished for questioning the orthodoxy of the group.

explanationZhTw: 這是 Newspeak 的自造詞，指所有被禁止的性行為被打包成同一類。
generatedExample: The policy treated every violation as SEXCRIME.

explanationZhTw: 這是 Newspeak 的自造詞，指被政權認可、符合規範的性行為。
generatedExample: In their system, GOODSEX was the only acceptable conduct.

explanationZhTw: 「一般性交」；在這裡是被允許的、只為生育服務的性行為。
generatedExample: The doctor used normal intercourse as the clinical term.

explanationZhTw: 「唯一目的只是生育」；強調這種行為被完全限制成繁殖用途。
generatedExample: They framed the rule as being for the sole purpose of begetting children.

explanationZhTw: 「肉體愉悅」；這裡特別指出女性不被允許享受快感。
generatedExample: The article discussed physical pleasure as part of human wellbeing.

explanationZhTw: 「異端思想」；在這裡指違反黨規、不能被允許延伸的想法。
generatedExample: Even a heretical thought could get him into trouble.

explanationZhTw: 「不存在的」；這裡指連表達那個想法所需的詞彙都沒有。
generatedExample: Without the right vocabulary, the concept feels nonexistent.

explanationZhTw: 委婉語；用比較不直接的說法包裝敏感、負面或不想直說的事物。
generatedExample: In customer service, we sometimes use euphemisms to soften bad news.

explanationZhTw: 在這裡指「幾乎完全相反的意思」；不是一般字面印象，而是故意反向命名。
generatedExample: The slogan sounded positive, but it meant almost the exact opposite.

explanationZhTw: 輕蔑的；帶有看不起、嘲諷的態度。
generatedExample: He gave a contemptuous smile when he heard the excuse.

explanationZhTw: 劣質的、垃圾般的；常用來批評內容很差、沒有價值。
generatedExample: They filled the channel with rubbishy reality shows.

explanationZhTw: 假的；不真實的；看起來像真的，但其實沒有根據。
generatedExample: The report was based on spurious claims and bad data.

explanationZhTw: 片語動詞，指「發放、分送」；這裡是黨把內容提供給大眾。
generatedExample: The teacher handed out the worksheets before class.

explanationZhTw: 矛盾的；有兩種相反傾向或感受。
generatedExample: She felt ambivalent about moving to another city.

explanationZhTw: 涵義；聯想意義。它不只是字面意思，還包含額外的情感或態度。
generatedExample: The word has a negative connotation in this context.

explanationZhTw: 乍看之下；初看時的印象。
generatedExample: At first sight, the plan looked simple.

explanationZhTw: 單純只是縮寫；mere 在這裡強調「只是、僅僅」，帶有貶抑或限制語氣。
generatedExample: These codes are mere abbreviations, not real explanations.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「fitted into」不是單純「放進去」，而是指把某件事納入某個系統、分類或範圍內。
generatedExample: The new rule was fitted into the existing workflow.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「cut down」是指縮短、壓縮，不是字面上的「砍倒」。
generatedExample: The article was cut down into a short summary for the newsletter.

explanationZhTw: 「as it were」是帶保留的說法，意思接近「可以說」「若這麼說的話」。
generatedExample: He became, as it were, the unofficial leader of the team.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「cling to」不是抓住實體物件，而是指某種聯想、印象或特質持續附著在某物上。
generatedExample: A sense of doubt still clung to his decision.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「call up」不是「打電話」，而是指喚起、引出某種影像、記憶或聯想。
generatedExample: That smell can call up memories of my childhood home.

explanationZhTw: 這是用來做對比的連接語，意思是「另一方面」。
generatedExample: The house is small. On the other hand, it is very cheap.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「taking thought」是較書面、較古典的說法，意思是「費心思考／仔細思量」。
generatedExample: She answered without taking thought for the consequences.

explanationZhTw: 「euphony」指「悅耳、好聽的聲音」；這裡是在說新語很重視讀起來或說起來順耳的程度。
generatedExample: The poet cared more about euphony than strict rhyme.

explanationZhTw: 「outweighed」在這裡是「比……更重要、勝過」；不是重量，而是指重要性超過其他因素。
generatedExample: Safety outweighed the cost of the trip.

explanationZhTw: 「exactitude」是「精確、準確」；整個片語指「意義必須非常精準」。
generatedExample: The editor demanded exactitude of meaning in every sentence.

explanationZhTw: 「sacrificed to」表示「為了某事而犧牲、放棄」；這裡是把文法一致性讓位給發音順口。
generatedExample: We sacrificed comfort to save time.

explanationZhTw: 「uttered rapidly」是「快速說出」；「utter」比一般的 say 更正式，常指把話說出口。
generatedExample: He uttered the warning rapidly and left the room.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「echoes」不是實際回音，而是指說話者腦中延伸出的聯想、反思或共鳴；整句意思是要讓人幾乎不去思考。
generatedExample: The slogan was designed to rouse the minimum of echoes in the listener's mind.

explanationZhTw: 「gained in force」是「變得更有力量、更有影響力」；這裡指因為詞彙都很相像，反而加強了效果。
generatedExample: His argument gained in force after he showed the evidence.

explanationZhTw: 「gabbling」指又快又含糊地喋喋不休；「gabbling style」就是雜亂、匆忙、難以清楚理解的說話方式。
generatedExample: Her gabbling style made the announcement hard to follow.

explanationZhTw: 「staccato」原本是音樂術語，指「斷奏、短促分離」；這裡形容說話一段一段、很短很急。
generatedExample: He spoke in a staccato rhythm during the interview.

explanationZhTw: 「monotonous」是「單調的、沒有變化的」；這裡形容語調聽起來很乏味、缺少起伏。
generatedExample: The lecture became monotonous after the first ten minutes.

explanationZhTw: 「ideologically neutral」指「在意識形態上中立的」；也就是不偏向任何政治或思想立場。
generatedExample: The report was written to sound ideologically neutral.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是一般的「獨立」，而是指說話幾乎不需要有意識思考就能自動產生。
generatedExample: The routine became independent of consciousness after years of practice.

explanationZhTw: 「reflect before speaking」是「說話前先思考」；這裡強調要先斟酌再開口。
generatedExample: I usually reflect before speaking when the topic is sensitive.

explanationZhTw: 「called upon to」在這裡是「被要求、被召喚去做」；語氣比普通的 asked to 更正式。
generatedExample: She was called upon to explain the decision at the meeting.

explanationZhTw: 「spray forth」是比喻用法，意思是「大量、連續地噴出」；這裡把說話比成機關槍掃射。
generatedExample: He sprayed forth facts so quickly that nobody could interrupt.

explanationZhTw: 「foolproof」指「幾乎不會出錯的、很可靠的」；這裡說語言像一種幾乎萬無一失的工具。
generatedExample: The checklist is foolproof if you follow it step by step.

explanationZhTw: 「texture」原意是「質地、紋理」；這裡用來指字詞本身的語感、聲音質感。
generatedExample: The texture of the words made the speech feel heavy and harsh.

explanationZhTw: 「in accord with」是「與……一致、符合」；比單純的 with 更正式。
generatedExample: The design was in accord with the company's values.

explanationZhTw: 「still further」表示「更加、進一步」；整個片語強調這些特徵又把這個過程推得更遠。
generatedExample: The new system assisted the process still further by removing delays.

explanationZhTw: 這裡指「可供選擇的範圍」；不是單純的地理區域，而是可做決定的空間。
generatedExample: A smaller menu gives customers less area of choice.

explanationZhTw: temptation 指「誘惑、衝動」，這裡是「想要去做某事的吸引力」。
generatedExample: He felt the temptation to reply immediately.

explanationZhTw: 這是較文學或古雅的說法，意思是「思考、動腦」。
generatedExample: She paused to take thought before answering.

explanationZhTw: issue from 表示「從某處湧出、發出」；這裡是在說話語像是從喉部自然冒出來。
generatedExample: A strange sound seemed to issue from the attic.

explanationZhTw: larynx 是「喉頭／喉部」，屬於較專門的解剖學用語。
generatedExample: The doctor examined the patient's larynx.

explanationZhTw: ambivalent 表示「含糊的、矛盾的、可有兩種解讀的」。
generatedExample: She felt ambivalent about moving to another city.

explanationZhTw: orthodox 指「正統的、符合主流或官方教條的」。
generatedExample: The company preferred orthodox methods over risky experiments.

explanationZhTw: orators 是「雄辯家、演說家」，指擅長公開演講的人。
generatedExample: The conference featured several skilled orators.

explanationZhTw: supplementary 指「補充的、附加的」，意思是它不是主要部分，而是用來補足其他內容。
generatedExample: This chapter is supplementary to the main textbook.

explanationZhTw: strip A of B 表示「把 A 的 B 去掉、剝奪掉」。這裡是把詞語中不想要的含義移除，只留下被允許的意思。
generatedExample: The editor stripped the paragraph of unnecessary jargon.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 currency 不是「貨幣」，而是「流通度、常用程度、被使用的普及性」。
generatedExample: That phrase still has currency among older speakers.

explanationZhTw: smattering 指「少量、粗淺的了解」；通常表示懂得不多，只會一點點。
generatedExample: I have only a smattering of Italian.

explanationZhTw: irrespective of 表示「不管、不考慮」；branches 在這裡是「分支、領域」的意思，不是樹枝。
generatedExample: The rule applies irrespective of your department's branches.

explanationZhTw: 「不合正統的觀點」；指偏離官方或主流立場的想法。
generatedExample: Unorthodox opinions are often rejected before people even hear the evidence.

explanationZhTw: 幾乎不可能；接近完全做不到。
generatedExample: The hike was well-nigh impossible in the storm.

explanationZhTw: 異端主張；在這裡指被正統立場視為錯誤的說法。
generatedExample: The pamphlet was burned because the authorities called it heresy.

explanationZhTw: 褻瀆；對神聖事物的冒犯。
generatedExample: Many people found the joke blasphemy, not humor.

explanationZhTw: 以正統觀點來聽的人；指非常遵循官方或傳統立場的人。
generatedExample: An orthodox ear would notice the change in doctrine immediately.

explanationZhTw: 不證自明的；一看就很明顯。
generatedExample: It was self-evident that the deadline had been missed.

explanationZhTw: 靠有條理的論證來支持或成立。
generatedExample: The claim could not be sustained by reasoned argument.

explanationZhTw: 對……有敵意的；與……相衝突的。
generatedExample: That policy is inimical to free expression.

explanationZhTw: 先以模糊、沒有語言的方式在腦中存在。
generatedExample: She entertained the idea in a vague wordless form for days.

explanationZhTw: 把……混在一起一概而論。
generatedExample: He lumped all the complaints together instead of sorting them.

explanationZhTw: 不正當地；不合規則地。
generatedExample: You cannot illegitimately copy code and call it original.

explanationZhTw: 明顯到幾乎可以感覺出的謊言或謬誤。
generatedExample: The article was full of a palpable untruth.

explanationZhTw: 清除掉；徹底刪除。
generatedExample: The old data was purged out of the system overnight.

explanationZhTw: 《一九八四》中的造詞，指同時接受互相矛盾的想法。
generatedExample: Orwell coined DOUBLETHINK to show how propaganda distorts thought.

explanationZhTw: 失誤；短暫的偏差。
generatedExample: A brief lapse in attention caused the error.

explanationZhTw: 次要或延伸的含義。
generatedExample: The phrase developed a secondary meaning over time.

explanationZhTw: 次要或延伸的含義。
generatedExample: The phrase developed a secondary meaning over time.

explanationZhTw: 超出他的能力範圍；他做不到。
generatedExample: It was beyond his power to fix the system alone.

explanationZhTw: 時間流逝；隨著時間過去。
generatedExample: With the passage of time, the rules became stricter.

explanationZhTw: 僵硬的；不易變通的。
generatedExample: His rigid routine left no room for spontaneity.

explanationZhTw: 《舊語》；在小說語境中指革命前的舊式語言，和「新語」相對。
generatedExample: Many older citizens still preferred Oldspeak in private conversations.

explanationZhTw: 被取代；被淘汰。這裡指舊語完全被新語取代。
generatedExample: The old system was superseded by a faster online process.

explanationZhTw: 切斷；斷絕。這裡是指和過去的連結被切斷。
generatedExample: The merger severed their long-standing business relationship.

explanationZhTw: 保留；維持。這裡是指只要還保有對舊語的知識，就還能讀懂那些碎片。
generatedExample: She retained enough Spanish to follow the conversation.

explanationZhTw: 無法理解的；看不懂的。
generatedExample: The note was handwritten so badly that it was unintelligible.

explanationZhTw: 無法翻譯的。這裡指那些文字即使存在，也不能再轉成新語。
generatedExample: Some jokes are untranslatable because they depend on wordplay.

explanationZhTw: 指涉；提到；談到。這裡是指文字內容所指向的事物。
generatedExample: The sentence referred to an event from last winter.

explanationZhTw: 正統的；符合官方標準的。這裡不是單純宗教意義，而是「符合黨的正統立場」。
generatedExample: Only orthodox opinions were considered safe to publish.

explanationZhTw: 小說中的新語造詞，表示「思想正確、符合正統」。
generatedExample: The poster repeated a GOODTHINKFUL slogan approved by the regime.

explanationZhTw: 在這裡指帶有意識形態目的的改寫式翻譯，不只是換語言，而是連意思也改掉。
generatedExample: The committee rejected what it called ideological translation of the original text.

explanationZhTw: 指「不證自明、顯而易見」，不需要再額外證明。
generatedExample: It is self-evident that the sun rises in the east.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是正式用法，表示「賦予、授與」某種特質或權利。
generatedExample: She is endowed with remarkable patience.

explanationZhTw: 指「不可剝奪的權利」，法律或政治語境中很常見。
generatedExample: Everyone should be protected by inalienable rights.

explanationZhTw: 指「追求幸福」；這是帶有政治哲學色彩的固定說法，不是單純日常用語。
generatedExample: For her, the pursuit of happiness means time with family and music.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 secure 不是「安全的」，而是「保障、確保」。
generatedExample: The new policy is designed to secure these rights for all citizens.

explanationZhTw: 指「設立、建立」制度或機構，語氣正式。
generatedExample: The committee was instituted to review the rules.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是「權力源自」的意思，deriving 表示從某處取得或引出。
generatedExample: The law derives its power from the constitution.

explanationZhTw: 指「被治理者的同意」，是政治哲學中的核心概念。
generatedExample: The idea of the consent of the governed shapes modern democracy.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 destructive 不是單純「破壞性的」形容，而是指「會損害、使之失效的」。
generatedExample: A system that is destructive of trust will not last long.

explanationZhTw: abolish 指「正式廢除、取消」制度、法律或機構；alter 是「修改」。
generatedExample: The city decided to alter the schedule rather than abolish it.

explanationZhTw: 這裡 institute 是「建立、設立」的正式用法，常見於制度或組織。
generatedExample: After the crisis, they chose to institute a new government.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `render` 不是「呈現」，而是「翻譯／改寫成」；`Newspeak` 指《1984》中的虛構政治語言，表示內容要被改寫成那套語言。
generatedExample: Can you render this paragraph into plain English?

explanationZhTw: `keep to` 在這裡是「忠於／遵守」的意思，表示翻譯時要盡量保留原意。
generatedExample: Please keep to the topic during the meeting.

explanationZhTw: `come to doing so` 表示「接近做到那件事、最多只能做到那個程度」。
generatedExample: The closest I could come to answering was to say I did not know.

explanationZhTw: `swallow up` 是比喻用法，表示「整個吞沒／全部吸收」；這裡指把整段文字濃縮成一個詞。
generatedExample: The company swallowed up its smaller rival last year.

explanationZhTw: 這是《1984》裡的虛構新語詞，指「犯罪性的思想」或違反官方思想的想法。
generatedExample: In the novel, CRIMETHINK is treated as a serious offense.

explanationZhTw: `ideological` 指「受意識形態驅動的」；整個片語表示翻譯不是中立傳達，而是替特定政治立場服務。
generatedExample: That slogan is more of an ideological translation than a neutral summary.

explanationZhTw: `panegyric` 是正式用語，表示「頌詞、讚歌」；整個片語意思是把文字改寫成一篇歌頌專制政權的文章。
generatedExample: The speech sounded like a panegyric on the new regime.

explanationZhTw: 名詞，指「聲望、地位、面子」。這裡表示出於政治或社會上的名聲考量。
generatedExample: The company acted out of prestige, not practicality.

explanationZhTw: 片語，意為「保留對……的記憶、讓人繼續記得」。
generatedExample: The museum was built to preserve the memory of the town's founders.

explanationZhTw: 慣用說法，意為「使符合、使一致」。這裡是把人物的成就改寫成符合某種意識形態。
generatedExample: The new policy brought the rules into line with international standards.

explanationZhTw: 片語，這裡不是一般的「翻譯過程」那麼簡單，而是指作品正在被系統性改寫成新語言或新版本。
generatedExample: Several ancient texts are now in process of translation.

explanationZhTw: 形容詞，意為「實用的；重功能、不講美感的」。
generatedExample: The room had a utilitarian design with no decoration.

explanationZhTw: 形容詞，意為「不可或缺的、必需的」。
generatedExample: A reliable translator is indispensable for the project.

explanationZhTw: 固定片語，意為「等等、諸如此類」。
generatedExample: We need pens, paper, notebooks, and the like.

explanationZhTw: 名詞片語，意為「前期工作、準備工作」。
generatedExample: The preliminary work took longer than expected.

explanationZhTw: 名詞，這裡指「正式採用、接納（某制度或語言）」。
generatedExample: The adoption of the new system will start next month.

explanationZhTw: 此處是「被定在／安排在」的意思，不是一般的「固定」物件。
generatedExample: The meeting was fixed for Friday afternoon.
